Defendants Legion Industries D/B/A Shield Corps Security, Stiver Bushgjokaj'S Motion To Compel The Continued Depositions Of Lyudmila Kuznetsova, Psy.D And Gabrielle Reiman, Psy.D
Off Calendar May-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Tanya Goberman, Esq. (415.545.8608; tanya@attorneytanya.com) for Plaintiff.
Michael Thomas, Esq. (415.474.1900) of Kern Segal & Murray for Defendants/Moving Party.

Judge Pro Tem Catalanotti states disclosure re: his current firm and previous firm's contact with parties in this case, that he did not handle cases with these parties, that it would not impact his impartiality and asked counsel whether they would like him to recuse himself. Ms. Goberman and Mr. Thomas stated they would present oral argument before Judge Pro Tem Catalanotti and does not ask him to recuse himself.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendants Legion Industries D/B/A Shield Corps Security, Stiver Bushgjokaj'S Motion To Compel The Continued Depositions Of Lyudmila Kuznetsova, Psy.D And Gabrielle Reiman, Psy.D:

Motion is GRANTED. The treating physicians at issue in the Motion are ordered to appear. Defendants to pay all costs associated with the depositions, including expert fees and costs of depositions. The parties are ordered to prepare a stipulated proposed order pursuant to the Court's instruction during oral argument. The proposed order should be submitted via email to the JPT by noon on 5/19 with all parties copied. If the parties cannot agree to the language in the proposed order, each party shall submit a proposed order via email to the JPT by noon on 5/19 with all parties copied, without argument.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
